# Local Daemon Implementation Contract Evidence

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/localdaemoncontract -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to invariants](../invariants.md)

Executable SSOT: [`local-daemon-contract.riido.json`](local-daemon-contract.riido.json).

Evidence artifact: `local-daemon-contract-evidence`.

Proves the public daemon runtime scheduling contract from executable code references instead of hand-written prose.

## Implemented Facts

| Fact | Summary | Evidence |
| --- | --- | --- |
| daemon exposes local task sources by env | cmd/riido owns the local queue and task DB source environment surface. | `daemon-env-task-queue`, `daemon-env-task-db` |
| supervisor owns runtime pool dispatch | daemon startup wires runtime actors into SupervisorActor and supervisor claims work by runtime id. | `supervisor-runtime-pool`, `supervisor-runtime-claim`, `supervisor-runtime-heartbeat` |
| supervisor gates provider start on accepted start report | provider preparation and execution do not begin when StartTask reporting is rejected. | `supervisor-start-report-gate` |
| supervisor retains terminal evidence until accepted | terminal task results remain in actor state and are retried when CompleteTask reporting is rejected. | `supervisor-terminal-report-retry` |
| supervisor retries low-volume state event evidence | stateful report events are retained and retried before terminal reports, while stream deltas stay out of the retry queue. | `supervisor-state-event-report-retry`, `supervisor-state-event-retention-policy` |
| supervisor drains shutdown terminal evidence within shutdown authority | shutdown cancellation results reuse the terminal retry path until reports are accepted or the lifecycle shutdown context expires. | `supervisor-shutdown-terminal-report-retry` |
| supervisor retains workspace warning evidence before terminal reports | task-time workspace ingest warning events use the retained event queue and must flush before terminal completion. | `supervisor-workspace-warning-retry` |
| supervisor retries runtime deregistration within shutdown authority | runtime registry deregistration is retried during shutdown until accepted or the lifecycle shutdown context expires. | `supervisor-runtime-deregister-retry` |
| scheduler selection is pure C5 logic | SelectRuntime orders candidates without locks, persistence, provider execution, or process side effects. | `scheduler-pure-selector`, `scheduler-runtime-pin` |
| task DB uses sidecar runtime registries | taskdbplane stores runtime registry and lease registry next to the task DB without changing the task DB schema. | `taskdb-runtime-registry-schema`, `taskdb-runtime-lease-schema`, `taskdb-runtime-registry-path`, `taskdb-runtime-lease-path` |
| task DB claims only the selected runtime | taskdbplane reuses SelectRuntime and only lets the selected runtime id claim the queued row. | `taskdb-select-runtime`, `taskdb-selected-runtime-only`, `taskdb-acquire-runtime-lease` |
| lease metadata fences reports | claimed TaskRequest metadata carries lease id, fencing token, and capability fingerprint; reports must match active lease identity. | `taskdb-lease-metadata`, `taskdb-require-runtime-lease`, `taskdb-reject-fencing-mismatch`, `taskdb-reject-fingerprint-mismatch` |
| expired leases requeue or terminate by state | expired Preparing/Running leases hand off through Blocked then Queued, while Claimed and NeedsInput become terminal. | `taskdb-expired-handoff`, `taskdb-expired-claimed-fails`, `taskdb-expired-needs-input-times-out` |
| heartbeats refresh only matching active leases | runtime heartbeats refresh running task leases only when runtime id and capability fingerprint still match. | `taskdb-heartbeat-refresh-runtime`, `taskdb-heartbeat-refresh-fingerprint` |

## Boundaries

| Boundary | Owner | Summary |
| --- | --- | --- |
| internal/scheduling | C5 runtime scheduling | pure runtime eligibility, selection, and runtime pin model |
| internal/agentbridge/supervisor | daemon runtime loop | runtime registration, claim dispatch, heartbeat, cancellation, and pre-submit eligibility |
| internal/agentbridge/controlplane/taskdbplane | local task DB adapter | task DB source/reporter, runtime registry sidecars, leases, fencing, and guarded task transitions |
| cmd/riido daemon | 12-factor local adapter | env/flag surface and runtime actor pool wiring |

## Absent Surfaces

| Surface | Scope | Reason |
| --- | --- | --- |
| scheduler has no adapter or persistence imports | `internal/scheduling` | C5 scheduling must stay pure and must not own daemon adapter, task DB, lease primitive, or HTTP behavior. |
| taskdbplane has no provider execution imports | `internal/agentbridge/controlplane/taskdbplane` | taskdbplane may claim/report tasks but must not execute provider processes. |

## Assertions

- local daemon scheduling claims are runtime-scoped, not process-global
- scheduler selection is pure and cannot acquire leases or spawn providers
- task DB runtime state is sidecar state, not a task DB schema expansion
- progress/result reports require active lease identity and fencing token evidence
- expired leases have explicit handoff or terminal outcomes
